etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 隹(隹) (zhuī, "short-tailed bird") -> original form of 鷕 -> borrowed phonetically for 'only'
etymology.interpretation: The character 唯 originally represented the cry of a bird, with the mouth radical indicating the sound aspect. It was later phonetically borrowed for the abstract meaning 'only', without retaining its original context related to bird sounds.

examples.chinese: 唯有努力(唯有努力)，才能成功(才能成功)。
examples.pinyin: Wéi yǒu nǔlì, cái néng chénggōng.
examples.english: Only by working hard can one succeed.

examples.chinese: 唯有在安静的夜晚(唯有在安靜的夜晚)，我才能听清自己的心声(我才能聽清自己的心聲)。
examples.pinyin: Wéi yǒu zài ānjìng de yèwǎn, wǒ cái néng tīng qīng zìjǐ de xīnshēng.
examples.english: Only in the quiet of the night can I hear my own heart.

examples.chinese: 我唯爱读书(我唯愛讀書)，因为书籍能带给我无限的可能(因為書籍能帶給我無限的可能)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ wéi ài dúshū, yīnwèi shūjí néng dài gěi wǒ wúxiàn de kěnéng.
examples.english: I only love reading, because books bring me infinite possibilities.
